vertical_split_selection.sort_order: descending by non_pk_column_count, then table name

base_predicate_sqls: ("AnswerText" = '1' OR ("AnswerText" = 'I don''t know' OR ("AnswerText" = 'None did' OR "AnswerText" = 'United States of America')))
("AnswerText" IS NULL OR "AnswerText" NOT IN ('-1', 'No', 'Yes', '1', '0', 'Maybe', 'I don''t know', 'None did', 'United States of America'))
fragment_roles: rows where AnswerText = Yes + rows where AnswerText = 0 + rows where AnswerText = Maybe
rows where AnswerText = 1 + rows where AnswerText = I don't know + rows where AnswerText = None did + rows where AnswerText = United States of America
rows where AnswerText is NULL or not in {'-1', 'No', 'Yes', '1', '0', 'Maybe', "I don't know", 'None did', 'United States of America'} (catch-all 'OTHER' bucket)

justification.cross_local_query_count_interpretation: Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.
Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.
Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.

relocation_engine_debug.pair_weight_meaning: Weights count co-occurrences of two fragments (from different original tables) inside workload queries that already span 2+ locals. They rank relocation candidates; they do not equal the number of queries that will become single-local after a move.
